blocks.body: Future Perfect — will have + V3 for actions that will already be complete by a certain moment or another event in the future.

{"level":"B1–B2","intro":"Future Perfect shows that an action will already be complete by a certain moment, deadline, or another event in the future.","rules":[{"label":"Forms","color":"emerald","text":"Start with the model will have + V3 for all subjects.","example":"I will have finished. / They will have left."},{"label":"Before a future point","color":"blue","text":"This tense focuses on the idea that the action will be ready by a future moment.","example":"By 6 p.m., we will have finished the report."},{"label":"Before another future event","color":"amber","text":"Future Perfect helps you show the order: first completion, then another future event.","example":"By the time you arrive, she will have gone home."},{"label":"Result by a deadline","color":"violet","text":"We often use it for deadlines, plans, and predictions of completion.","example":"By next month, they will have opened the new office."}]}

{"title":"1. Lessons in order","intro":"Start with the form and use, then move to negatives, questions, and time expressions. Each card includes short rules from the matching lesson.","items":[{"label":"B1–B2","title":"Forms and Use","subtitle":"will have + V3 and the main meanings of Future Perfect.","rules":[{"label":"Form","formula":"will have + V3","text":"The form is the same for every subject: will have + V3.","example":"I will have finished by 6."},{"label":"Use","formula":"completed by then","text":"Use it for an action that will be complete before a future moment.","example":"By Friday, they will have left."}]},{"label":"B1–B2","title":"Negatives","subtitle":"will not have / won't have + V3 for actions that will not be complete by that point.","rules":[{"label":"Negative","formula":"won't have + V3","text":"Use will not / won't have + V3 for negatives.","example":"We won't have finished by then."},{"label":"Yet / never","formula":"not completed","text":"This shows that an action will not be complete before a deadline or moment.","example":"She won't have arrived before lunch."}]},{"label":"B1–B2","title":"Questions and Short Answers","subtitle":"Will you have finished...? / Yes, I will. / No, I won't.","rules":[{"label":"Question","formula":"Will + subject + have + V3?","text":"Put Will at the beginning and keep have before V3.","example":"Will you have finished by 6?"},{"label":"Short answers","formula":"will / won't","text":"Repeat will or won't in short answers.","example":"Yes, I will. / No, she won't."}]},{"label":"B1–B2","title":"Time Expressions","subtitle":"by, by the time, before, by tomorrow evening, by then, already.","rules":[{"label":"Result now","formula":"by / by the time","text":"By shows the point before which the action will be complete.","example":"I will have finished by tomorrow."},{"label":"Open period","formula":"before / by then","text":"These markers also focus on completion before a future moment.","example":"They will have left by then."}]}]}

{"title":"2. Where you need this tense","sections":[{"label":"Deadlines and targets","color":"emerald","description":"Talk about something that will be complete by a specific date, time, or deadline.","examples":[{"en":"By Friday, I will have sent all the invoices.","ua":"Everything will already be sent by Friday."},{"en":"By tomorrow evening, we will have packed everything.","ua":"The preparation will already be done by then."}]},{"label":"Before another future event","color":"blue","description":"Show that one action will finish earlier than another future action or moment.","examples":[{"en":"By the time the guests arrive, we will have cooked dinner.","ua":"Dinner will be ready first, then the guests will arrive."},{"en":"She will have finished the meeting before the train leaves.","ua":"Completion comes before the other event."}]},{"label":"A ready result","color":"amber","description":"Future Perfect works well when the important point is that the result will already be there.","examples":[{"en":"By then, he will have learned enough English for the interview.","ua":"The needed result will already be ready by that point."},{"en":"The builders will have completed the roof before winter.","ua":"That stage of work will already be complete before winter."}]},{"label":"Predictions of completion","color":"violet","description":"The tense often appears in predictions about what will be done by a certain future time.","examples":[{"en":"By next year, the company will have opened three new stores.","ua":"We predict a completed result by a future limit."},{"en":"In two years, scientists will have developed better tools.","ua":"We look ahead and imagine a result that is already complete."}]}]}

{"title":"3. In short","items":["Future Perfect = will have + V3.","This tense shows that an action will already be complete by a certain moment or event in the future.","It works well for deadlines, plans, order of events, and predictions of completion.","Words like by, by the time, before, by then, and already often point to this tense."]}
